title: Pecluma hoehnii
intro: Pecluma hoehnii, also known as Hoehne's Pecluma or Bird's Nest Fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to Brazil. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pecluma hoehnii include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pecluma hoehnii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Pecluma hoehnii is classified in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody fern family. This family is a part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all types of ferns. The genus Pecluma is further classified under the order Polypodiales, which comprises various types of polypody ferns. Within the order, Pecluma hoehnii is distinct due to its unique characteristics and distribution. Further classification of Pecluma hoehnii involves its specific species classification within the genus.
